subject: [RFC PATCH 2/2] virtio-balloon: add stats push mode

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary: Raised concerns about potential blocking operations in the virtio-balloon stats push mode, suggesting that the issue might be related to CH's interfaces or a misunderstanding of the interaction.
reviews.raw_body:
On Tue, Jun 16, 2026 at 05:52:29PM +0200, David Hildenbrand (Arm) wrote:
> On 6/16/26 16:44, Gregory Price wrote:
> > That makes sense, although don't you just push the blocking operation
> > into yet another thread on the host?
> 
> I think timers are run from the QEMU main thread, so no separate thread just for
> the timer.
> 
> And IIRC, there will be no blocking. At least if I understand your concern
> correctly.
> 
> balloon_stats_poll_cb() will do a virtqueue_push()+virtio_notify(), which will
> notify the device. The main thread will continue afterwards doing what a main
> thread usually does.
> 
> A VCPU will process the request in the VM and send it back + notify the device.
>

Entirely possible I just bungled the interaction then and/or CH's
interfaces introduce a blocking op that shouldn't.

Thanks for the feedback, we can probably drop this patch.  Unless
there's any particular pushback for 1/2, should i leave as-is or
resubmit separately w/o RFC?

~Gregory

reviews.author: David Hildenbrand (Arm)
reviews.summary: Clarified that timers are run from the QEMU main thread and that there should be no blocking operations in the virtio-balloon stats push mode.

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary: Acknowledged the potential issue and considered revising or withdrawing the patch, but asked if there was any particular pushback for the first part of the patch.
reviews.raw_body:
On Tue, Jun 16, 2026 at 05:52:29PM +0200, David Hildenbrand (Arm) wrote:

Thanks for the feedback, we can probably drop this patch.  Unless

2026-06-16.patch_summary: The patch adds a stats push mode to virtio-balloon, but concerns were raised about potential blocking operations.
